input.series_concept: A three-part documentary series about the hidden infrastructure of global food supply chains — how the food on a supermarket shelf traveled thousands of miles through warehouses, container ships, cold chains, and sorting facilities staffed by some of the lowest-paid workers in the economy.
episode_focus: Ep 1: The harvest — migrant farmworkers on industrial fruit farms, the first link in the chain
Ep 2: The journey — container shipping, cold chain logistics, warehouse workers, just-in-time systems
Ep 3: The shelf — supermarket economics, food waste, the gap between what consumers see and what the chain actually looks like
input.target_broadcaster: International streaming platform and European public broadcasters

input.series_concept: A documentary series about people who build their own homes from scratch, with no prior construction experience
episode_focus: Ep 1-6: Each episode follows one self-builder from foundation to completion

input.series_concept: A four-part documentary series about the four surviving national railways of Europe — France (SNCF), Germany (Deutsche Bahn), Italy (Trenitalia), and Spain (Renfe) — and how the privatisation, neglect, or investment decisions made over three decades have shaped the daily experience of hundreds of millions of passengers and the economic geography of the continent.
episode_focus: Ep 1: France — TGV and the two-speed railway: high-speed glamour vs. crumbling rural lines
Ep 2: Germany — Deutsche Bahn in crisis: delays, infrastructure debt, and the political failure to invest
Ep 3: Italy — Trenitalia and Italo: what happens when you introduce competition on the rails
Ep 4: Spain — Renfe and the AVE: the most ambitious railway expansion in Europe, built on EU structural funds
input.comparable_series: All Aboard! (Channel 4), The Railway: Keeping Britain on Track (BBC)
input.budget_tier: Mid-to-high — requires filming across four countries with translation

input.series_concept: A six-part documentary series about Africa's megacity generation — young people aged 18-30 building lives in Lagos, Nairobi, Kinshasa, Addis Ababa, Dar es Salaam, and Luanda. Each city is growing by over 1 million people per year. The series follows one young person in each city as they navigate housing, work, creativity, and community in urban environments that formal planning has largely abandoned.
episode_focus: Ep 1: Lagos — a fashion designer building a brand from a single sewing machine in Yaba
Ep 2: Nairobi — a software developer in Silicon Savannah whose parents are pastoralists in rural Kenya
Ep 3: Kinshasa — a musician navigating the world's most vibrant music scene with no formal industry infrastructure
Ep 4: Addis Ababa — an architect trying to preserve historic buildings as the city demolishes entire neighborhoods
Ep 5: Dar es Salaam — a woman running a boda-boda delivery fleet in a city with no address system
Ep 6: Luanda — a tech entrepreneur building an app for a country where mobile data costs more than food
input.target_broadcaster: Al Jazeera English, Netflix Africa, or BBC Africa Eye
input.comparable_series: Welcome to Lagos (BBC), City of Joy (Netflix)
input.budget_tier: Mid — requires filming crews in 6 countries over 12+ months

input.series_concept: Something about the ocean. Maybe three or four episodes. For TV.
